sourceMovie.title: I, Tonya
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: I, Tonya (2017) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Craig Gillespie's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: mid90s
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If I, Tonya clicked for you, mid90s (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after I, Tonya?
faq.answer: Try mid90s, To Die For, Nocturnal Animals—each shares DNA with I, Tonya while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is I, Tonya?
faq.answer: I, Tonya (2017) is best described as Drama, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is I, Tonya worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
